_comment: Reference template. The canonical location for your real config is `$SKVM_CACHE/skvm.config.json` (default `~/.skvm/skvm.config.json`); `skvm config init` writes there with chmod 0600. This template is also read from `<project>/skvm.config.json` for backwards compat. The adapters map points each external agent at a local checkout. Omit an adapter to let skvm fall through to `which <name>` on PATH (and, for opencode only, the skvm-bundled binary as a final fallback).
_providersComment: `providers.routes` is the single source of truth for model-id → backend routing. EVERY CLI-level model id must carry a `<provider>/` prefix — e.g. `openrouter/qwen/qwen3-30b`, `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6`, `openai/gpt-4o`, `ipads/gpt-4o`. The prefix is stripped by skvm before the backend SDK sees the id (so OR gets `qwen/qwen3-30b`, OpenAI gets `gpt-4o`, etc.). Routes cover: in-process LLM calls (compiler, judges, bare-agent adapter, jit-boost candidate parsing) AND the skvm-internal headless agent used by jit-optimize / jit-boost (opencode subprocess — skvm auto-injects OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT when the matched route is openai-compatible). External adapters (opencode/openclaw/hermes/jiuwenclaw used as bench/profile/run targets) get standard SDK env vars (OPENAI_API_KEY/OPENAI_BASE_URL/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/OPENROUTER_API_KEY) derived from the route, but their own provider/model config (opencode.jsonc / openclaw models.json / jiuwenclaw .env) is the user's responsibility. Routes match top-to-bottom; first glob (literal + * wildcard) match wins. Each route needs ONE of: `apiKey` (literal, stored here — gitignored + chmod 0600 when written by `skvm config init`) or `apiKeyEnv` (env var name). The wizard writes `apiKey` by default; switch to `apiKeyEnv` if you keep keys elsewhere (direnv / vault / CI).
_headlessAgentComment: Optional, minimal. The headless agent (opencode subprocess used internally by jit-optimize / jit-boost) resolves credentials and endpoints automatically from providers.routes — there is no separate provider-override knob. Use this section only to pin `driver` or to force a specific `opencodePath`; unset fields fall through to skvm defaults.
